item.title: HarbourVOICES! Festival Underway in Capital City
item.dc:creator: Allison King
item.description:
Oh b'y, the town's alive with music this week, it is! HarbourVOICES! be bringin' in hundreds of singers from all over tarnation. They kicked 'er off on the weekend, and there's still lots to come over the next few days, if ye got the ear for it. There's more than 40 groups here, including the Phoenix Chamber Choir all the way from Vancouver.
Nicole Andrews, she's the director there, and wouldn't ya know it, she's one of our own from St. John's. She's pretty chuffed to be part of this big ol' music hullabaloo. Claims it's as grand as Festival 500 from donkey's years ago. But she says ya gotta see it to believe it. Sounds grand and all, but let's hope they don't drown out the sound of me trap fishin' radio!
item.content:encoded:
Oh b'y, the town's alive with music this week, it is! HarbourVOICES! be bringin' in hundreds of singers from all over tarnation. They kicked 'er off on the weekend, and there's still lots to come over the next few days, if ye got the ear for it. There's more than 40 groups here, including the Phoenix Chamber Choir all the way from Vancouver.
Nicole Andrews, she's the director there, and wouldn't ya know it, she's one of our own from St. John's. She's pretty chuffed to be part of this big ol' music hullabaloo. Claims it's as grand as Festival 500 from donkey's years ago. But she says ya gotta see it to believe it. Sounds grand and all, but let's hope they don't drown out the sound of me trap fishin' radio!

item.title: Mount Pearl to Break Ground on New Community Centre This Week
item.dc:creator: Allison King
item.description: Oh b'y, ya won't believe it: Mount Pearl's at it again, breakin' ground on some fancy new community centre this week. Remembah where the old Smallwood Arena used to be? Yeah, slap near the Team Gushue Complex on Arena Road. They're plannin' to do the deed on Wednesday at noon. Guess it's somethin' to keep the townies busy, eh? Hope they throw in a decent fishin' pond this time—might actually get some use out of the thing!
item.content:encoded: Oh b'y, ya won't believe it: Mount Pearl's at it again, breakin' ground on some fancy new community centre this week. Remembah where the old Smallwood Arena used to be? Yeah, slap near the Team Gushue Complex on Arena Road. They're plannin' to do the deed on Wednesday at noon. Guess it's somethin' to keep the townies busy, eh? Hope they throw in a decent fishin' pond this time—might actually get some use out of the thing!

item.title: Man Arrested Following Assault at St. John’s Restaurant
item.dc:creator: Allison King
item.description: Well, b'ys, listen to this racket. Some feller in St. John's decided it'd be a grand idea to start a ruckus at a restaurant on Sunday evenin'. Right durin' supper, no less! The RNC had to get involved, and turns out the feller they nabbed, he's 28 and already has a rep for not followin' court orders. So now he's charged with assault with a weapon. Sounds like this fella's got the common sense of a jellybean!
item.content:encoded: Well, b'ys, listen to this racket. Some feller in St. John's decided it'd be a grand idea to start a ruckus at a restaurant on Sunday evenin'. Right durin' supper, no less! The RNC had to get involved, and turns out the feller they nabbed, he's 28 and already has a rep for not followin' court orders. So now he's charged with assault with a weapon. Sounds like this fella's got the common sense of a jellybean!

item.title: Unknown Soldier Laid to Rest at National War Memorial

media:content.media:description: Pallbearers bring the unknown soldier to the tomb at the National War Memorial in downtown St. John's. July 1, 2024

item.dc:creator: Allison King
item.description:
Oh b'y, you missed this, did ya? They finally put the Unknown Soldier to rest right here in Newfoundland and Labrador, down by the National War Memorial in St. John’s. There were thousands out there, even the big wigs like Trudeau, Mary Simon, and our own Premier Furey, who's apparently the next of kin or somethin'. Shocker.
The ceremony was something else, though. They did this big 19-gun salute and had a Hercules plane fly over. It nearly jarred the nerve outta me, but I suppose it’s fitting, right. They placed 820 forget-me-not flowers on the casket too, all sentimental like.
This poor soul had quite the journey, mind you. Started off in France in May, over at the Beaumont-Hamel site, with the Regiment Caribou Memorial. Then they brought him home and laid his remains in state at Confederation Building for three days. Gotta say, if you wanted to go gawk, you had plenty of time.
This morning, they took the casket through downtown Water Street to the monument, with folks turning out and getting all emotional. It’s meant to be a tribute to all our Newfoundlanders and Labradorians who fought in the Great War but never made it back. There wasn't a dry eye in the place, I’d say.
Good on 'em for the decent send-off, but you gotta wonder how much it all cost, eh?
item.content:encoded:
Oh b'y, you missed this, did ya? They finally put the Unknown Soldier to rest right here in Newfoundland and Labrador, down by the National War Memorial in St. John’s. There were thousands out there, even the big wigs like Trudeau, Mary Simon, and our own Premier Furey, who's apparently the next of kin or somethin'. Shocker.
The ceremony was something else, though. They did this big 19-gun salute and had a Hercules plane fly over. It nearly jarred the nerve outta me, but I suppose it’s fitting, right. They placed 820 forget-me-not flowers on the casket too, all sentimental like.
This poor soul had quite the journey, mind you. Started off in France in May, over at the Beaumont-Hamel site, with the Regiment Caribou Memorial. Then they brought him home and laid his remains in state at Confederation Building for three days. Gotta say, if you wanted to go gawk, you had plenty of time.
This morning, they took the casket through downtown Water Street to the monument, with folks turning out and getting all emotional. It’s meant to be a tribute to all our Newfoundlanders and Labradorians who fought in the Great War but never made it back. There wasn't a dry eye in the place, I’d say.
Good on 'em for the decent send-off, but you gotta wonder how much it all cost, eh?

item.title: Canadian Armed Forces Explains Why Canadian Flag is Draped Over Casket of Unknown Soldier

media:content.media:description: The unknown soldier lying-in-state at Confederation Building. Photo via Gov NL.

item.dc:creator: Allison King
item.description:
Ah b'y, so here's what 'appened. There's been some fuss about the Canadian Armed Forces usin' the maple leaf flag on the casket of an unknown soldier from WWI. See, some folks are riled up 'cause WWI was before Newfoundland joined Canada in '49, so they reckon he should've had a Newfoundland flag instead.
But the Armed Forces fired back sayin' they're only allowed to use the Canadian flag on the caskets. Plus, they consider any missing Newfoundland soldiers as Canadian post-Confederation. Bit of a head-scratcher if ya ask me, like tryin' to fit a square peg in a round hole. Makes ya wonder if they’re just sweepin’ the ol’ Newfoundland history under the rug, eh?
item.content:encoded:
Ah b'y, so here's what 'appened. There's been some fuss about the Canadian Armed Forces usin' the maple leaf flag on the casket of an unknown soldier from WWI. See, some folks are riled up 'cause WWI was before Newfoundland joined Canada in '49, so they reckon he should've had a Newfoundland flag instead.
But the Armed Forces fired back sayin' they're only allowed to use the Canadian flag on the caskets. Plus, they consider any missing Newfoundland soldiers as Canadian post-Confederation. Bit of a head-scratcher if ya ask me, like tryin' to fit a square peg in a round hole. Makes ya wonder if they’re just sweepin’ the ol’ Newfoundland history under the rug, eh?

item.title: Police Looking for Wanted Man Who Fled Scene of Accident in Arnold’s Cove
item.dc:creator: Allison King
item.description:
Ah, b'ys, you won't believe this one. So in Arnold's Cove on the weekend, there was this fella, Jeremy Branton, right? He's 31 and apparently caused a right mess. Rammed his car right into a motorcyclist. Poor lad on the bike got pretty banged up and then Jeremy just up and bolted, leaving the poor guy to fend for himself.
Now, they say Jeremy's got a load of charges comin' his way. Criminal negligence, dangerous operation of a car, breach of probation—y'know, the whole nine yards. Guess he didn't think about keepin' the rubber down, let alone waitin' around to face the music. Cheeky bugger thought he could just vanish, but the Clarenville RCMP are on the hunt. Some folks never learn, eh?
item.content:encoded:
Ah, b'ys, you won't believe this one. So in Arnold's Cove on the weekend, there was this fella, Jeremy Branton, right? He's 31 and apparently caused a right mess. Rammed his car right into a motorcyclist. Poor lad on the bike got pretty banged up and then Jeremy just up and bolted, leaving the poor guy to fend for himself.
Now, they say Jeremy's got a load of charges comin' his way. Criminal negligence, dangerous operation of a car, breach of probation—y'know, the whole nine yards. Guess he didn't think about keepin' the rubber down, let alone waitin' around to face the music. Cheeky bugger thought he could just vanish, but the Clarenville RCMP are on the hunt. Some folks never learn, eh?

item.title: WestJet Reaches Deal with Mechanics to End Strike
item.dc:creator: Allison King
item.description:
B'y, you wouldn't believe the foolishness going on with WestJet. So, they finally struck a deal with them mechanics who were on strike and mucked up everybody's Canada Day plans. Real mess that was, eh? Anyways, they're sayin' it'll be a rough week getting things back to normal. Most of the flights in and out of St. John's today are still cancelled, so if you're flying, better check before you haul your arse to the airport.
The union, Airplane Mechanics Fraternal Association or whatever they're called, is telling their members to get back to work right away while they figure out this temporary agreement. About 680 of 'em walked off last Friday evening. Since Thursday, they cancelled 829 flights, and that's during the busiest weekend of the season. Ain't that just grand? So everyone’s been stuck trying to figure out how to get where they're going. Typical, eh?
item.content:encoded:
B'y, you wouldn't believe the foolishness going on with WestJet. So, they finally struck a deal with them mechanics who were on strike and mucked up everybody's Canada Day plans. Real mess that was, eh? Anyways, they're sayin' it'll be a rough week getting things back to normal. Most of the flights in and out of St. John's today are still cancelled, so if you're flying, better check before you haul your arse to the airport.
The union, Airplane Mechanics Fraternal Association or whatever they're called, is telling their members to get back to work right away while they figure out this temporary agreement. About 680 of 'em walked off last Friday evening. Since Thursday, they cancelled 829 flights, and that's during the busiest weekend of the season. Ain't that just grand? So everyone’s been stuck trying to figure out how to get where they're going. Typical, eh?

item.title: Veterans Affairs Minister Pays Tribute to Unknown Soldier

media:content.media:description: Photo via Ginette Petitpas Taylor on X

item.dc:creator: Allison King
item.description:
Well, b'ys, the Minister of Veterans Affairs, Ginette Petitpas Taylor, was out an' about in St. John's this weekend, makin' a big to-do 'bout an unknown Newfoundland soldier from World War I. She's been sayin' these memorial events have been a long time comin', eh?
On Saturday, she had herself a grand ol' time at The Rooms, goin' through the Beaumont-Hamel and Trail of the Caribou exhibits. She even took a gander at the National War Memorial, the Royal Newfoundland Regiment Museum, and hit up the Crows Nest too. I s'pose she was playin' tourist for the day.
Then on Sunday, she went over to the Confederation Building to have a private look-see at the unknown soldier lying in state. Emotional business, she says.
She’s right proud, reckonin’ it's somethin' special seein' Canadians turnin' up for these kinds of memorials. She noted how Newfoundlanders got lots of pride when it comes to bringin' their own back home, which she saw firsthand at the War Memorial.
Anyhow, now that lad is gettin’ laid to rest proper at the base of the Newfoundland National War Memorial. So, cheers to her for the visit, but honestly, feels like a lot of pomp and circumstance, doesn’t it?
item.content:encoded:
Well, b'ys, the Minister of Veterans Affairs, Ginette Petitpas Taylor, was out an' about in St. John's this weekend, makin' a big to-do 'bout an unknown Newfoundland soldier from World War I. She's been sayin' these memorial events have been a long time comin', eh?
On Saturday, she had herself a grand ol' time at The Rooms, goin' through the Beaumont-Hamel and Trail of the Caribou exhibits. She even took a gander at the National War Memorial, the Royal Newfoundland Regiment Museum, and hit up the Crows Nest too. I s'pose she was playin' tourist for the day.
Then on Sunday, she went over to the Confederation Building to have a private look-see at the unknown soldier lying in state. Emotional business, she says.
She’s right proud, reckonin’ it's somethin' special seein' Canadians turnin' up for these kinds of memorials. She noted how Newfoundlanders got lots of pride when it comes to bringin' their own back home, which she saw firsthand at the War Memorial.
Anyhow, now that lad is gettin’ laid to rest proper at the base of the Newfoundland National War Memorial. So, cheers to her for the visit, but honestly, feels like a lot of pomp and circumstance, doesn’t it?

item.title: NL to Honour Unknown First World War Soldier with Historic Interment at National War Memorial
item.dc:creator: Allison King
item.description:
Well b'y, today's a big deal down in St. John's, let me tell ya. They're finally buryin’ an unknown World War I soldier at the National War Memorial. It’s been years in the makin’, bringin' his remains back from France and fixin' up the memorial for its 100th anniversary. First time they showed it off was way back in 1924, can ya believe it?
They’re goin' all out for the fella – with a full military funeral, yer Last Post, a moment of silence, gun salutes, and even a flyby from the Air Force, if the weather plays nice. And all the big wigs, includin' the Prime Minister, will be there too. Sure, why not add a bit more pomp and circumstance, eh?
They say this unknown soldier represents all the Newfoundlanders and Labradorians who bit the bullet in wartime. Premier Furey’s been at the helm of this effort since he got into office, and he’s claimin’ this is the most important thing he’s done in his life, aside from marryin’ his missus and havin' kids. Quite the stretch there, but you gotta admire the sentiment, don't ya?
Anyway, after all the hullabaloo of buryin’ the soldier, they’re havin' a Memorial Day Commemorative Ceremony. So if ya got nothin' better to do, maybe take a gander downtown and show your respects.
item.content:encoded:
Well b'y, today's a big deal down in St. John's, let me tell ya. They're finally buryin’ an unknown World War I soldier at the National War Memorial. It’s been years in the makin’, bringin' his remains back from France and fixin' up the memorial for its 100th anniversary. First time they showed it off was way back in 1924, can ya believe it?
They’re goin' all out for the fella – with a full military funeral, yer Last Post, a moment of silence, gun salutes, and even a flyby from the Air Force, if the weather plays nice. And all the big wigs, includin' the Prime Minister, will be there too. Sure, why not add a bit more pomp and circumstance, eh?
They say this unknown soldier represents all the Newfoundlanders and Labradorians who bit the bullet in wartime. Premier Furey’s been at the helm of this effort since he got into office, and he’s claimin’ this is the most important thing he’s done in his life, aside from marryin’ his missus and havin' kids. Quite the stretch there, but you gotta admire the sentiment, don't ya?
Anyway, after all the hullabaloo of buryin’ the soldier, they’re havin' a Memorial Day Commemorative Ceremony. So if ya got nothin' better to do, maybe take a gander downtown and show your respects.

item.title: Capital City Holding Canada Day Festivities; Fireworks Cancelled
item.dc:creator: Allison King
item.description:
Oh me nerves... So, b'y, here's the scoop on them Canada Day shenanigans in town, right? They started off at the crack of dawn up on Signal Hill – probably wakin' the gulls, no doubt. Then they got a whole pile of family-friendly things lined up for this afternoon at King George V Park. Ya know, games, live music, and all that jazz – it's free, but they’re asking folks to bring some non-perishables for the food bank, which is a bit of common decency, if you ask me.
But, get this, the live music and fireworks they was hyping up for tonight? Cancelled. Just like that. Probably some foolishness with the weather or somethin'. So, there ya have it – bit of a half celebration if you ask me. Wonder how many folks will actually haul up there now. Typical!
item.content:encoded:
Oh me nerves... So, b'y, here's the scoop on them Canada Day shenanigans in town, right? They started off at the crack of dawn up on Signal Hill – probably wakin' the gulls, no doubt. Then they got a whole pile of family-friendly things lined up for this afternoon at King George V Park. Ya know, games, live music, and all that jazz – it's free, but they’re asking folks to bring some non-perishables for the food bank, which is a bit of common decency, if you ask me.
But, get this, the live music and fireworks they was hyping up for tonight? Cancelled. Just like that. Probably some foolishness with the weather or somethin'. So, there ya have it – bit of a half celebration if you ask me. Wonder how many folks will actually haul up there now. Typical!
